## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so now we're going to talk about joint venture launches. So joint venture launches, they can get pretty complex, but at the most basic, what it is is a joint venture partner of yours or an affiliate, they will mail their list to go and look at your pre-launch content, then the people from their list will go through your pre-launch, buy your product, and then you'll give that joint venture partner some portion of the sales, the proceeds, the commission. And so the great thing about this is you can really, it's just a way to all of a sudden put yourself in front of a lot of people, because just think about it, you might have a list of a thousand people that you've built up over months or a year or whatever, but if you can find a partner that's got 10,000 people on their list, it's like, wow, all of a sudden you have extended your influence in a big way, and you can, I mean joint venture launches can be really, really lucrative. I've personally made millions and millions and millions, in fact, I have tens of millions of dollars in sales in affiliate, in JV launches, really, really powerful stuff. Now, it sounds awesome, but there's a lot of gotchas, and it's not like a quick fix in general. So what I'm going to give you is here in Product Launch Formula, as you've seen throughout all of Product Launch Formula, it's all about the long term, having a long term win, it's not a get rich quick, and you can't look at joint venture partners as a get rich quick type of thing. This is very much a holistic approach to your business. It's a longer term approach, but it absolutely works. So the first thing you've got to do is you've got to build out your internal launch first, and you need to get your metrics and make sure it works. So let's draw this out. The circle of awesome, which I covered earlier in Product Launch Formula, is this idea where you do your internal launch first, you figure out what your metrics are, you make sure the launch works, and then you move forward and you do a joint venture launch. Because what a joint venture launch is, is okay, if this is your internal launch, then let's just say you have a joint venture partner out here with a list, what they would do is they would mail into a squeeze page, a squeeze page, and the people that opted in would

get your first piece of pre-launch content. Now just think if you had like five of those joint venture partners, and they each had those 10,000 person lists, and they're all mailing into your squeeze page, you can see how this can get very exciting very quickly. This is the absolute best way to build a list, because these people that come through your squeeze page, they're now on your list. What you're going to do is when you're going to track via software, and we'll get into that a little bit later, but once they join your list and they run through these pieces of prelaunch content, if they buy from you, then you are going to give your partner's commission based on the sales that you make to the people that sent to you. But the one thing you got to do is you got to do the internal launch first, so you get the metrics right. You don't want to ever test a launch on your partner's list, because if you put together a launch, and the launch is not a great offer, it's not a great prelaunch, or maybe your systems aren't in place, and your shopping cart isn't hooked up right, or some technical problem. If you make a mistake with a joint venture launch, these partners, probably you're going to burn your relationship with them, and they're not going to come back and promote for you in the long run. ## [06:05 - 09:05]

Now one of the things you want to do, of course, to do any of this is you have to get these partners on board. And so that's what we're going to talk about the rest of this video and the next video, and I think the next video after that, because this is a really key thing, is getting the partners so they're willing to mail for you. So if someone's got a 10,000-person list, they've got a lot of people coming to them asking if they'll promote for them. That's just the way it goes. So how do you find partners? This might sound really simplistic, but Google is one of the greatest ways. Well, frankly, if you're in a market and you've been in that market for a while, you probably have a good idea of who your competitors are. And I use that term, competitors. I really think of them, and not as competitors, but as future partners. So who in your market are the big dogs? Who are the people that are publishing? Who are the people that are building lists? Then so create a list of those. I use a spreadsheet to do this. Then go to Google and do a search on your main keyword phrases and go and look at the top 20 or the top 50 listings and visit every one of those sites and see are they building a list? If they're not building a list, they're probably not going to be a great partner, a great venture partner, a great affiliate, but are they building a list? Go ahead and join their list. You probably want to put together, get a separate email account or separate Gmail account so you can join all their lists and watch what they're doing. Then go in and join their affiliate programs. Do they have an affiliate program? When you go to their website, just do a search on their page and see if they've got an affiliate program. Join their affiliate program. Watch what they send out to their affiliates. Watch what they send out to their main list. Then like I said, create that spreadsheet and track them. Track who promotes who, who's sending good email, who's not doing a very good job of following up. You really want to understand what the JV landscape is like within your market. Are these top five all promoting each other? Or are they mostly not? People aren't promoting each other. You really need to focus in on that overall JV landscape. Then other ways to find them is once you get that list of potential partners in Google, another way is to go to live events. I'm a big fan of going to live events to meet potential partners. You need to basically go there and you're looking for who the players are, who the people that have lists and are getting results. Go to ClickBank and search on their marketplace. This doesn't work for all products and all markets, but a lot of them, you can take a look at the marketplace and ClickBank and see what products are selling. They have this measure called gravity and that basically tells you what products are selling well. You can go in there and see who's got really good products that are selling really well.

## [09:05 - 11:35]

These are just some of the ways that you can start to figure out who are the players, who can really make an impact in your market. One of the things is, like I said, I want you to go through the top 50 sites and find out who's building the list and start building relationships. The reality is, you only need one partner. I drew this out first with this one partner. I added in four more, but you can do a nice JV launch with just one partner. Even great launches, great, great launches can be done with just five really committed partners. I'm talking million dollar launches. If you get the right five partners, they'll end up bringing in some other people on their coattails, but you need those five that are really committed. To start out, you really only need one that is really committed. Getting these people on board, the thing is, everyone is too busy these days. Everyone is. But entrepreneurs are even more busy than anyone else. Successful entrepreneurs are completely way too busy. You've got to break through that and build a relationship with them if you're going to get them to promote for you. Building a relationship in this business is no different from any other business out there. You have to add value to them. If you don't have enough partners, then you just haven't added enough value to enough other people's lives yet. You've got time. The way you do this is one relationship at a time. The next video is going to be all about building those relationships. The only thing you really want to avoid is what I call this T-Bone JV marketing. That's like, sometimes I'll go to an event and I'll feel like I've got a couple of T-Bone steaks tied around my legs. The other people, then there'll be people out there, they're like pit balls that want me to promote for them. They see me as a piece of meat. They're just coming at me and they just want to eat those T-Bones that are tied to me. After me, this pit ball after this T-Bone, not the way to do it. You don't want to just be rushing up to someone and just asking them, can you mail for me, mail for me, mail for me, mail for me. It doesn't work. You have to build relationships. You have to build value in other people's lives. That's what we're going to get to in the next video.
